finding_results.evidence: chapters/chapter_14.md:125; chapters/chapter_14.md:131; chapters/chapter_14.md:133; chapters/chapter_14.md:135; chapters/chapter_14.md:137; chapters/chapter_14.md:139
finding_results.notes: Kai now accesses the forensic trace through emergency audit mode on his own session tree, initially mistakes the local target for crash hardware, then confirms theft through duplicated session IDs, raw payload manifests, and the repeated qadir_y operator signature. The text also makes clear why ordinary athlete view hid the routing and why Petra's team has not yet reviewed the older captures while Tomas is still in imaging.
Yusuf's confession now moves through stressed technical framing and private-register pressure instead of echoing the style-bible exemplar's admission-plus-justification-plus-cutoff scaffold.
The replacement line drops the 'yeah + admission + nobody else would have it + self-interruption' template and avoids the leaked justification logic.
The chapter now re-enters through Tomas's group-session focus and witnessed hurdle work instead of repeating rubber-smell, ceiling-height, and footfall-acoustics inventory.
Only two glasses references remain, preserving the strongest technical-mode beat and removing the repeated put-on/take-off/rub cycle from the confrontation.
Dense He/His sentence openings were recast across the crash, aftermath, and discovery sections. Post-revision check: He = 17 of 403 sentences (4.2%), His = 5 of 403 sentences (1.2%).
Kai's earlier blackout crash is now dated to six weeks ago, aligning forward with chapter 11 rather than shrinking backward in time.
The revision adds only consequential material in Kai's discovery and confrontation run while preserving the heading contract. Post-revision word count is 5020.

finding_results.evidence: chapters/chapter_14.md:195; chapters/chapter_14.md:199; chapters/chapter_14.md:203; chapters/chapter_14.md:205; chapters/chapter_14.md:209; chapters/chapter_14.md:241
finding_results.notes: Roughened both targeted dialogue zones in the Kai-Yusuf confrontation. (1) Yusuf's code explanation at line 195 now self-corrects mid-list rather than delivering a clean organized readout; the composed summary 'It reads like something they'd ship' replaced with blunter 'It's their code.' (2) Yusuf's virus-origin claim at line 199 now breaks mid-sentence ('somebody with access to their —') and restarts with a colloquial restart ('Their systems, yeah'), satisfying the acceptance test's requirement for at least one visible interruption or self-correction. (3) Kai's Tomas line at 203 now trails off unfinished ('and he still —') and lands on the physical fact 'He's twenty-one' rather than delivering a composed moral summary. (4) The three-part thesis slogan 'The virus makes the surrender rational, the patch is the reward, and the dependency is the point' at line 205 replaced with Yusuf spelling out the dependency mechanism in rougher, longer, less compressed phrasing. (5) Kai's polished moral summation 'Tomas doesn't need your standard of evidence' at line 241 replaced with a physically grounded accusation ending in a real question ('What were you waiting for?'). All four must-land beats, three tracked payoffs, and chapter state shift preserved. No new continuity introduced.

pass_summaries.summary: Reworked the archive reveal into an earned forensic-trace discovery, rewrote Yusuf's confession away from the style-bible exemplar, corrected the crash timeline, trimmed repeated scene-entry and glasses tics, reduced pronoun-opener density, and expanded the chapter with consequential pressure so it now clears the 5000-word floor.

Single finding addressed: the Kai-Yusuf confrontation's dialogue roughened across both targeted passages. Removed the three-part thesis slogan, two composed moral summations, and one clean mini-speech. Added self-interruption, unfinished turns, and concrete room-grounded accusations. Same facts remain on the table (code-structure similarity, server-authorized suppression, Kai's anger about Tomas, Yusuf's inability to prove intent) but now delivered through pressured turn-taking rather than composed diagnosis.
